questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution is the Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG) appointed?
Women are guaranteed a minimum of _____ of seats in Panchayats.
Which of the following is a goal of the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.option_3: Promotion of international peace and security

questions.question: Which of the following financial powers is NOT vested in the Governor of a state?
questions.option_4: Presenting the State Budget to the Legislature

questions.question: Which of the following features introduced by the 73ʳᵈ Constitutional Amendment ensures the continuous functioning of Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs)?
questions.option_1: Creation of Lok Adalats at Panchayat level
questions.option_2: Elections within six months of dissolution of a Panchayat
questions.option_3: Inclusion of Panchayati Raj in the Union List
questions.option_4: Provision for direct funding from the Union government

questions.question: The division of subjects between the Union and the States is laid out in which Schedule of the Constitution?
Why is Sanskrit counted among the modern Indian languages by the Constitution of India?
questions.option_1: Due to its status as the most ancient classical language
questions.option_2: Given its designation as India's national language
questions.option_3: As it serves as the linguistic root for all contemporary Indian languages
questions.option_4: Because it continues to be actively used

questions.question: How did Public Interest Litigation (PIL) emerge in India as a tool of judicial activism?
questions.option_1: It began in 1979 when the Supreme Court allowed individuals to file cases for public interest.
questions.option_2: It was introduced by the Lok Adalat system in 2005.
questions.option_3: It originated under the 42ⁿᵈ Constitutional Amendment (1976).
questions.option_4: It was first used during the Emergency (1975-77).

questions.question: Which amendment granted constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions?
Which of the following commissions was appointed to look into the question of states based on language?
The __________ of the Indian Constitution protects laws from being challenged in courts under judicial review.
Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution specifically deal with the establishment of Tribunals for administrative and other matters?
What is the mandated time for the Public Information Officer (PIO) to provide information?
questions.option_3: 30 days generally and 48 hours for life or liberty cases

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment granted constitutional status to Co-operative Societies in India?
In which Schedule are the forms of oath and affirmation for various constitutional offices specified?
Which constitutional body is empowered to adjudicate legal disputes between the Centre and one or more states in India?
Which of the following Directive Principles reflects Gandhian ideology?
questions.option_3: Separation of judiciary from the executive

questions.question: Which legal authority gives statutory status to Lok Adalats in India?
Which Article of the Constitution of India deals with the post of the Governor?
Which Amendment is considered a near rewrite of the Constitution and made changes to the Preamble, Schedule VII and 53 Articles?
Which type of Municipality is formed for a transitional area under Article 243Q of the Indian Constitution?
Which Article gives the President of India the power to grant pardons and reprieves?
Which of the following duties was added by the Constitution (Eighty-sixth Amendment) Act, 2002?
questions.option_3: To uphold and protect national unity
questions.option_4: To provide education to children aged six to fourteen years

questions.question: What is the nature of the Concurrent List in the Indian Constitution?
questions.option_1: Both the Centre and the States can legislate

questions.question: In India, how many sessions of Parliament are held in a year?
